title: caragana frutex subsp. mollis
intro: Caragana frutex subsp. mollis, also known as the smooth pea shrub, is a type of flowering shrub native to Central Asia, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and woody stems. Traditionally, Caragana frutex subsp. mollis has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing, due to its bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. Caragana frutex subsp. mollis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional.
